title: Becium dhofarense

This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Becium dhofarense belonging to the tribe Teucrideae. It is a member of the Becium genus, which is closely related to other species of plants in the same region.

intro: Becium dhofarense, also known as East African blue basil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in East African medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, as well as its antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Becium dhofarense include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the leaves of Becium dhofarense are used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and pharmaceutical preparations.
